popularStory.headline: Death of Sir William "Le Hardi" Douglas
popularStory.description:
Sir William 'Le Hardi' of Douglas was the son of Sir William of Douglas. He died in 1298 at London, England, as a prisoner.

Citations

1. [S6] G.E. Cokayne; with Vicary Gibbs, H.A. Doubleday, Geo …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Lord William "Le Hardi" Douglas 5th Laird of Douglas於1243年出生在蘇格蘭藍納可爾道格拉斯 卡素時，他的父親William "Longlegs" Douglas 3rd Lord of Douglas23歲，母親Constance Battaille of Fawdon28歲。他於1289年1月28日與Eleanor Lovaine在蘇格蘭藍納可爾道格拉斯結婚。他們至少是3個兒子的父母。他去世於1298年1月24日在英國米德萊可堂爾 本 倫敦享年55歲，埋葬在蘇格蘭藍納可爾道格拉斯 卡素。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lord William "Le Hardi" Douglas 5th Laird of Douglas于1243年出生在苏格兰藍納可爾道格拉斯 卡素时，他的父亲William "Longlegs" Douglas 3rd Lord of Douglas23岁，母亲Constance Battaille of Fawdon28岁。他于1289年1月28日与Eleanor Lovaine在苏格兰藍納可爾道格拉斯结婚。他们至少是3个儿子的父母。他去世于1298年1月24日在英国米德萊可堂爾 本 倫敦享年55岁，埋葬在苏格兰藍納可爾道格拉斯 卡素。

nameMeanings.name: Douglas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from any of various places called from their situation on a river named with Gaelic dubh ‘dark, black’ + glas ‘stream’ (a derivative of glas ‘blue’). There are several localities in Scotland and Ireland so named, but the one from which the surname is derived in most if not all cases is Douglas in Lanarkshire 20 miles south of Glasgow, the original stronghold of the influential Douglas family and their retainers.

History: The family taking their name from Douglas in Lanarkshire were of Flemish origin. They rose to great prominence in the 14th and 15th centuries, controlling the earldoms of Douglas, Morton, and Angus, and later, Queensberry.

variants.name: Douglass
meanings: Scottish and English (Durham and Northumbria): variant of   Douglas  .

History: William Douglass, a physician recognized for his identification and description of an epidemic of scarlet fever, was born c. 1691 in Gifford, Haddington County, Scotland, and settled in Boston in 1718. The abolitionist, orator, and journalist Frederick Douglass assumed the name after escaping from slavery in 1838 and traveling to Massachusetts. Son of a white father and a slave with some Indian blood, he was born Frederick Augustus Washington Bailey c. 1817 in Tuckahoe, MD.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

meanings: ‘Pertaining to praise and gratitude’ (in particular for Allāh's favours).

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: When Lord William "Le Hardi" Douglas 5th Laird of Douglas was born in 1243, in Douglas Castle, Lanarkshire, Scotland, his father, William "Longlegs" Douglas 3rd Lord of Douglas, was 23 and his mother, Constance Battaille of Fawdon, was 28. He married Eleanor Lovaine on 28 January 1289, in Douglas, Lanarkshire, Scotland. They were the parents of at least 3 sons. He died on 24 January 1298, in Tower of London, Middlesex, England, at the age of 55, and was buried in Douglas Castle, Lanarkshire, Scotland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lord William "Le Hardi" Douglas 5th Laird of Douglas est né en 1243, à Douglas Castle, Lanarkshire, Écosse, son père, William "Longlegs" Douglas 3rd Lord of Douglas, avait 23 ans et sa mère, Constance Battaille of Fawdon, avait 28 ans. Il a épousé Eleanor Lovaine le 28 janvier 1289, à Douglas, Lanarkshire, Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils. Il est décédé le 24 janvier 1298, à Tower of London, Middlesex, Angleterre, à l’âge de 55 ans, et a été enterré à Douglas Castle, Lanarkshire, Écosse.

de.lifeSummary: Als Lord William "Le Hardi" Douglas 5th Laird of Douglas 1243 in Douglas Castle, Lanarkshire, Scotland geboren wurde, war sein Vater William "Longlegs" Douglas 3rd Lord of Douglas 23 und seine Mutter Constance Battaille of Fawdon 28. Er hat am 28. Januar 1289 in Douglas, Lanarkshire, Scotland Eleanor Lovaine geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne. Er starb am 24. Januar 1298 im Alter von 55 Jahren in Tower of London, Middlesex, England und wurde in Douglas Castle, Lanarkshire, Scotland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Lord William "Le Hardi" Douglas 5th Laird of Douglas è nato il nel 1243 nel Douglas Castle, Lanarkshire, Scotland, il suo padre, William "Longlegs" Douglas 3rd Lord of Douglas, aveva 23 anni e il suo madre, Constance Battaille of Fawdon, aveva 28 anni. Lui sposato Eleanor Lovaine il 28 gennaio 1289, nel Douglas, Lanarkshire, Scotland. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli. Morì il 24 gennaio 1298, nel Tower of London, Middlesex, England, all'età di 55 anni, e fu sepolto a Douglas Castle, Lanarkshire, Scotland.

ja.Douglas, Lanarkshire, Scotland: スコットランドラナルクシュアイアドウグラス
ja.lifeSummary: Lord William "Le Hardi" Douglas 5th Laird of Douglasが1243年にスコットランドラナルクシュアイアドウグラス カストルで生まれた時，父William "Longlegs" Douglas 3rd Lord of Douglasは23歳，母Constance Battaille of Fawdonは28歳であった。1289年1月28日にスコットランドラナルクシュアイアドウグラスでEleanor Lovaineと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子の親であった。彼は1298年1月24日にイギリスミッドレセックストウエル オフ ロンドンで55歳で死去し，スコットランドラナルクシュアイアドウグラス カストルにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Lord William "Le Hardi" Douglas 5th Laird of Douglas(이)가 1243년 스컷랜드 랜아르크실르 더글라스 카스틀에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William "Longlegs" Douglas 3rd Lord of Douglas(은)는 23세였고, 그의 어머니 Constance Battaille of Fawdon(은)는 28세였다. 그는 1289년 1월 28일 스컷랜드 랜아르크실르 더글라스에서 Eleanor Lovaine(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1298년 1월 24일 영국 미들레세스 타워 프 런던에서 55세의 나이로 사망했으며 스컷랜드 랜아르크실르 더글라스 카스틀에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Lord William "Le Hardi" Douglas 5th Laird of Douglas nasceu em 1243, em Douglas Castle, Lanarkshire, Scotland, seu pai, William "Longlegs" Douglas 3rd Lord of Douglas, tinha 23 anos e sua mãe, Constance Battaille of Fawdon, tinha 28 anos. Ele casou-se com Eleanor Lovaine em 28 de janeiro de 1289, em Douglas, Lanarkshire, Scotland. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos. Ele faleceu em 24 de janeiro de 1298, em Tower of London, Middlesex, Inglaterra, com 55 anos, e foi sepultado em Douglas Castle, Lanarkshire, Scotland.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lord William "Le Hardi" Douglas 5th Laird of Douglas родился в 1243 г. в местности доуглас кастл, ланаркшир, Шотландия, его отцу по имени William "Longlegs" Douglas 3rd Lord of Douglas было 23 года, а матери, которую звали Constance Battaille of Fawdon, 28. Он женился на женщине по имени Eleanor Lovaine 28 января 1289 г. в местности доуглас, ланаркшир, Шотландия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына. Он умер 24 января 1298 г. в местности тоуир оф лондон, миддлесекс, Англия в возрасте 55 лет и был похоронен в местности доуглас кастл, ланаркшир, Шотландия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Lord William "Le Hardi" Douglas 5th Laird of Douglas nació en 1243, en Douglas Castle, Lanarkshire, Scotland, su papá, William "Longlegs" Douglas 3rd Lord of Douglas, tenía 23 años y su mamá, Constance Battaille of Fawdon, tenía 28 años. Se casó con Eleanor Lovaine el 28 de enero de 1289, en Douglas, Lanarkshire, Scotland. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos. Murió el 24 de enero de 1298, en Tower of London, Middlesex, Inglaterra, a la edad de 55 años, y fue sepultado en Douglas Castle, Lanarkshire, Scotland.
